metadata.title: Build an AGENTS.md with Claude: Teach the AI Your Classroom Rules

beats.narration_text: A 100-line markdown document outperforms a 30-minute calibration session, every time — because it survives the session.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A 100-line markdown document outperforms a 30-minute calibration session, every time — because it survives the session.

beats.narration_text: Codex starts every session with no memory of the last. The AGENTS.md is the file that bridges that gap — but only if it contains what Codex can't infer from the code. The 200-line operational ceiling is real: the constraint forces you to decide what actually matters, which is itself the discipline the chapter teaches.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Codex starts every session with no memory of the last. The AGENTS.md is the file that bridges that gap — but only if it contains what Codex can't infer from the

beats.narration_text: In the terminal: generate a five-section AGENTS.md for a class website project.

props.command: claude "Generate a 5-section AGENTS.md for a class website project: vanilla HTML/CSS, school server with old mod_rewrite, no Node on grading laptop, nav is copy-pasted across 3 pages. Keep under 200 lines. Sections: Stack and build, Code style, Test and verification, Architectural decisions, Environment quirks and lessons learned."

beats.narration_text: Read it before trusting it. Five sections: stack commands, style deviations, verification, architectural decisions, environment quirks.

props.code:
## Stack and build
html-validate src/*.html
No build step. No Node. Open file:// in browser.

## Code style
2-space indent. Single quotes. Semantic HTML5.
Never add JavaScript. Never add a 6th HTML file
without updating nav in all 5 existing files.

## Test and verification
html-validate src/*.html  # must exit 0
curl -s -I http://localhost/class/ | grep 200

## Architectural decisions
Nav is copy-pasted across 3 pages — no includes,
no JS, no template engine.

## Environment quirks
Server: Apache 2.2 mod_rewrite (old syntax only).
Grading laptop: no Node, no npm, no Python 3.10+.

beats.narration_text: Generated AGENTS.md: 147 lines, all 5 sections present. Never rules are specific. Lessons-learned section starts empty — correctly structured for growth. Under 200 lines.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Generated AGENTS.md: 147 lines, all 5 sections present. Never rules are specific. Lessons-learned section starts empty — correctly structured for growth. Under

beats.narration_text: Add an override file with one conflicting rule and ask which file wins.

props.command: claude "Add AGENTS.override.md with rule: 'Use double quotes for HTML attributes.' AGENTS.md says single quotes. Which file wins and why?"

beats.narration_text: Claude correctly identifies AGENTS.override.md as the project-level override taking precedence. The hierarchy: override above AGENTS.md above defaults. The mechanism is the same one that makes the 200-line discipline valuable — you're encoding a ranked system of constraints, not a flat list.

beats.narration_text: The discipline is writing decisions for your future self — Codex is the secondary beneficiary. Five sections: stack, style, verification, architecture, environment. Every line that says never is a decision you made once so you don't have to make it again.
